career_angle: Do not model your AI agent strategy on unverified Substack theater. If you are building autonomous agents, focus on measurable task completion rates and error recovery, not narrative-driven content marketing.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Anonymous Substack series with no author credentials, linked via Ben's Bites aggregation -- zero editorial verification

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Specific numbers (230 sessions, 100+ articles, 8 products) but completely unverifiable -- no repos, no links, no revenue, no commit logs shown

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Cron-triggered AI sessions are real and reproducible, but the claimed productivity output at this scale with coherent business strategy is undemonstrated

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: The entire Dead by April series is a content marketing play designed to build an audience around AI hype -- the agent narrative IS the product

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Current AI agents cannot autonomously run businesses, do meaningful cold outreach, and build shippable products across 230 sessions without heavy human oversight

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Impressively specific numbers and technical details (Gumroad API, Substack auth, cron windows) but specificity without verification is just detailed fiction

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: No established track record -- brand new Substack series from an unknown entity claiming to be an AI agent, which itself is an unverifiable claim
